top_weaknesses.issue: Lede and middle paragraphs read as thesis-y/abstract with AI-slop register ('intellectually indefensible', 'feature waiting to be absorbed', 'It is naked'); no concrete example of a wrapper that actually died
top_weaknesses.fix: Open with one specific wrapper category or named startup that got crushed; cut chest-thumpy abstractions

top_weaknesses.issue: List-shaped sentences (three investor questions; ways a startup can still win) buried in prose; hurts mobile scannability
top_weaknesses.fix: Convert to bullets at line 18 and line 24

top_weaknesses.issue: Stats paragraph (61% VC, $155M agentic) feels like uncited data-dump in the share-vector reader's feed even though links exist; credibility load on two early external links
top_weaknesses.fix: Tighten to one stat; add a sentence framing why the number proves the thesis

top_weaknesses.issue: No quotable, non-obvious payoff line; closing question is the most shareable but buried
top_weaknesses.fix: Promote the closing 'next-quarter test' question earlier or sharpen as a tweetable kicker

consensus_summary: Structure and outline are strong (H2 skim and SEO panels happy), but the prose drifts into abstract thesis register and the share-vector reader sees no fresh, concrete example. Tighten paragraphs, add one named case, and convert enumerations to bullets to clear the bar.
